title: alyssum campestre subsp. hirsutum
intro: Alyssum campestre subsp. hirsutum, also known as hairy alyssum, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae, characterized by its hairy stems and leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of hairy alyssum include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and even exhibit antimicrobial properties. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
